justification: B correctly excludes GraphQL (read-only queries written by others, no real implementation) and VS Code/Chrome DevTools (tools, not skills), landing on exactly the four validated skills: JavaScript, HTML, CSS, Git. A incorrectly includes GraphQL despite the candidate only running pre-written read-only queries, and also includes VS Code and Chrome DevTools which inflate the skill list beyond what is warranted. B also provides proper project classification (both correctly labeled 'Frontend Only') and extracts project details, while A provides no project data at all. Impact quality is roughly equal for both since the resume contains no real numeric metrics — B's internship duration note is marginally concrete but still vague. B wins decisively on skill_accuracy and conservatism, and adds value with correct project classification.
